meta.title: Glioma-Related Epilepsy Predictive Factors
meta.cff_info:
Glioma-Related Epilepsy Predictive Factors

Li et al., 2021

DOI: 10.3171/2020.12.JNS203351

Factors and Points

  • Age:
    • 2.4 points: Age <45 years
    • 0 points: Age ≥45 years
  • Functional Deficit:
    • 0.2 points: Absent
    • 0 points: Present
  • Preoperative Epilepsy:
    • 10 points: Present
    • 0 points: Absent
  • Extent of Resection (EOR):
    • 3.5 points: Non-GTR
    • 0 points: GTR
  • Tumor Location (Temporal Lobe Involvement):
    • 4.2 points: Involved
    • 0 points: Non-involved
  • WHO Grade:
    • 3.3 points: Grade III
    • 0 points: Grade IV
  • IDH Mutation:
    • 1 point: Present
    • 0 points: Absent
  • Ki-67 Expression:
    • 1.6 points: ≤10%
    • 0 points: >10%

Risk Estimates

Cumulative risk of seizure 12 months post-surgery based on total score:

Score Risk

Visualization

Figure 2: A complex nomogram for predicting PTE.

Additional Study Information

Category Details
Population 449 patients stratified by IDH mutation and 1p/19q codeletion status.
Statistical Methods Multivariate logistic regression and Cox proportional hazards regression.
Validation Methods Retrospective cohort, single-center study.
Performance Metrics No AUC or c-statistics reported. Performance is evaluated using odds ratios for specific factors.
Generalizability Moderate (limited to single-center cohort, specific to glioma-related epilepsy).
Clinical Utility Guides AED use and seizure management strategies in glioma patients.
meta.authors: Li L, Fang S, Li G, et al.
meta.clinical_utility: OBJECTIVE The aim of this study was to investigate the epidemiological characteristics, associated risk factors, and prognostic value of glioma-related epilepsy in patients with diffuse high-grade gliomas (DHGGs) that were diagnosed after the 2016 updated WHO classification was released. METHODS Data from 449 patients with DHGGs were retrospectively collected.
meta.outcome: Glioma-related epilepsy in patients with diffuse high-grade glioma after the 2016 WHO update: seizure characteristics, risk factors, and clinical outcomes

predictors.name: Age
predictors.assess: Determined at diagnosis. Younger patients (<45 years) are at higher risk of glioma-related epilepsy. OR for <45 years: 2.6 (95% CI: 1.6–4.3, p < 0.001).

predictors.name: Functional Deficit
predictors.assess: Defined as impairments in sensorimotor, language, or vision functions, including sensory abnormalities, decreased muscle strength, aphasia, and visual field defects. OR for normal neurological function: 3.0 (95% CI: 1.7–5.3, p < 0.001).

predictors.name: Preoperative Epilepsy
predictors.assess: Defined as at least one unprovoked epileptic seizure before surgery. OR for preoperative epilepsy: 7.6 (95% CI: 4.0–14.2, p < 0.001).

predictors.name: Extent of Resection (EOR)
predictors.assess: Classified based on postoperative MRI as Gross Total Resection (GTR: no residual tumor signal) or Non-GTR (incomplete tumor removal). OR for Non-GTR: 2.3 (95% CI: 1.2–4.3, p = 0.012).

predictors.name: Tumor Location (Temporal Lobe Involvement)
predictors.assess: Defined as involvement of the temporal lobe. OR for temporal lobe involvement: 2.0 (95% CI: 1.1–3.6, p = 0.033).

predictors.name: WHO Grade
predictors.assess: umor classification per WHO standards. Lower-grade tumors (Grade III) are more epileptogenic. OR for Grade III: 2.1 (95% CI: 1.1–4.0, p = 0.021).

predictors.name: IDH Mutation
predictors.assess: Mutation in the Isocitrate Dehydrogenase (IDH) gene. OR for IDH mutation: 1.7 (95% CI: 0.9–3.0, not significant in multivariate analysis).

predictors.name: Ki-67 Expression
predictors.assess: A proliferation marker indicating tumor growth. OR for low Ki-67 expression: 1.6 (significant in univariate analysis only, p = 0.042).

provenance.notes: Predictors/points/rows from CFF export; complete meta from paper; verify vs live form.
